 1	# Farm Connect - Fresh, Local Food Marketplace
 2	
 3	[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
 4	[![Node.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node.js-18+-green.svg)](https://nodejs.org/)
 5	[![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-18+-blue.svg)](https://reactjs.org/)
 6	[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5+-blue.svg)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
 7	
 8	> **Connecting local farmers with conscious consumers for fresh, sustainable food delivery**
 9	
 10	Farm Connect is a modern, full-stack web application that bridges the gap between local farmers and consumers, promoting sustainable agriculture and fresh food access. Built with cutting-edge technologies and featuring a comprehensive admin dashboard, secure payment processing, and real-time features.
 11	
 12	## Live Demo
 13	
 14	- **Main Application**: [http://localhost:5000](http://localhost:5000)
 15	- **Admin Dashboard**: [http://localhost:5000/admin-login](http://localhost:5000/admin-login)
 16	 - Username: `admin`
 17	 - Password: `123456`
 18	
 19	## ✨ Key Features
 20	
 21	### **E-commerce Platform**
 22	- **Product Catalog**: Browse fresh produce from local farms
 23	- **Shopping Cart**: Add, remove, and manage items
 24	- **Secure Checkout**: Multiple payment methods with form validation
 25	- **Order Management**: Track orders from placement to delivery
 26	
 27	### **Authentication System**
 28	- **Multi-provider Login**: Google, Facebook, Phone number authentication
 29	- **User Profiles**: Customer and farmer account management
 30	- **Protected Routes**: Secure access to checkout and admin areas
 31	- **Session Management**: Persistent login sessions
 32	
 33	### **Advanced Payment Processing**
 34	- **Multiple Payment Methods**:
 35	 - Credit/Debit Cards (with real-time formatting)
 36	 - UPI Payment (with ID validation)
 37	 - Digital Wallets (Paytm, Amazon Pay, Mobikwik)
 38	 - Net Banking (major Indian banks)
 39	 - Cash on Delivery
 40	- **Form Validation**: Real-time validation with user feedback
 41	- **Order Confirmation**: Detailed order tracking and email notifications
 42	
 43	### ‍ **Farmer Features**
 44	- **Farmer Registration**: Verification with image proof and farm videos
 45	- **Product Management**: Add and manage farm products
 46	- **Profile Verification**: Image-based farmer verification system
 47	
 48	### **Professional Admin Dashboard**
 49	- **Real-time Monitoring**: Live system metrics and user activity
 50	- **6 Comprehensive Tabs**: Overview, Analytics, Users, Products, Orders, System
 51	- **Advanced Analytics**: Performance metrics, device analytics, trend tracking
 52	- **User Management**: Search, filter, and manage users
 53	- **System Health**: CPU, memory, disk usage monitoring
 54	- **Security Dashboard**: Login attempts, API requests, system logs
 55	
 56	## ️ Technology Stack
 57	
 58	### **Frontend**
 59	- **React 18** - Modern UI library with hooks
 60	- **TypeScript** - Type-safe development
 61	- **Vite** - Fast build tool and dev server
 62	- **Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first CSS framework
 63	- **Shadcn/ui** - Modern component library
 64	- **Wouter** - Lightweight routing
 65	- **React Hook Form** - Form management
 66	- **Zod** - Schema validation
 67	
 68	### **Backend**
 69	- **Node.js** - JavaScript runtime
 70	- **Express.js** - Web application framework
 71	- **TypeScript** - Server-side type safety
 72	- **Session Management** - Secure authentication
 73	- **RESTful APIs** - Clean API architecture
 74	
 75	### **Development Tools**
 76	- **ESLint** - Code linting
 77	- **Prettier** - Code formatting
 78	- **Hot Module Replacement** - Fast development
 79	- **Cross-env** - Environment variables
 80	
 81	## Installation & Setup
 82	
 83	### **Prerequisites**
 84	- Node.js 18+ installed
 85	- npm or yarn package manager
 86	- Git for version control
 87	
 88	### **Quick Start**
 89	
 90	1. **Clone the Repository**
 91	 ```bash
 92	 git clone https://github.com/your-username/farm-connect.git
 93	 cd farm-connect
 94	 ```
 95	
 96	2. **Install Dependencies**
 97	 ```bash
 98	 npm install
 99	 ```
 100	
 101	3. **Start Development Server**
 102	 ```bash
 103	 npm run dev
 104	 ```
 105	
 106	4. **Access the Application**
 107	 - Main App: [http://localhost:5000](http://localhost:5000)
 108	 - Admin Panel: [http://localhost:5000/admin-login](http://localhost:5000/admin-login)
 109	
 110	### **Available Scripts**
 111	
 112	```bash
 113	# Start development server
 114	npm run dev
 115	
 116	# Build for production
 117	npm run build
 118	
 119	# Preview production build
 120	npm run preview
 121	
 122	# Run linting
 123	npm run lint
 124	
 125	# Type checking
 126	npm run type-check
 127	```
 128	
 129	## Project Structure
 130	
 131	```
 132	farm-connect/
 133	├── client/ # Frontend React application
 134	│ ├── src/
 135	│ │ ├── components/ # Reusable UI components
 136	│ │ │ └── ui/ # Shadcn/ui components
 137	│ │ ├── context/ # React context providers
 138	│ │ ├── hooks/ # Custom React hooks
 139	│ │ ├── pages/ # Application pages
 140	│ │ ├── lib/ # Utility functions
 141	│ │ └── types/ # TypeScript type definitions
 142	│ ├── public/ # Static assets
 143	│ └── index.html # HTML template
 144	├── server/ # Backend Express application
 145	│ ├── index.ts # Server entry point
 146	│ └── routes.ts # API routes
 147	├── package.json # Dependencies and scripts
 148	├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript configuration
 149	├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind CSS configuration
 150	└── vite.config.ts # Vite configuration
 151	```
 152	
 153	## Configuration
 154	
 155	### **Environment Variables**
 156	Create a `.env` file in the root directory:
 157	
 158	```env
 159	NODE_ENV=development
 160	PORT=5000
 161	SESSION_SECRET=your-secret-key
 162	```
 163	
 164	### **Database Configuration**
 165	The application currently uses in-memory data storage. For production, configure your preferred database:
 166	
 167	```typescript
 168	// server/database.ts
 169	export const dbConfig = {
 170	 host: process.env.DB_HOST,
 171	 port: process.env.DB_PORT,
 172	 database: process.env.DB_NAME,
 173	 // ... other config
 174	};
 175	```
 176	
 177	## UI Components & Design
 178	
 179	### **Design System**
 180	- **Color Palette**: Green and blue gradients representing nature and trust
 181	- **Typography**: Clean, readable fonts with proper hierarchy
 182	- **Spacing**: Consistent spacing using Tailwind's spacing scale
 183	- **Components**: Fully accessible components with proper ARIA labels
 184	
 185	### **Responsive Design**
 186	- **Mobile-first**: Optimized for mobile devices
 187	- **Tablet Support**: Adaptive layouts for tablet screens
 188	- **Desktop**: Full-featured desktop experience
 189	- **Grid System**: Flexible grid layouts using CSS Grid and Flexbox
 190	
 191	## Authentication & Security
 192	
 193	### **Authentication Methods**
 194	1. **Google OAuth**: Secure Google account integration
 195	2. **Facebook Login**: Facebook social authentication
 196	3. **Phone Authentication**: SMS-based verification
 197	4. **Admin Login**: Secure admin panel access
 198	
 199	### **Security Features**
 200	- **Session Management**: Secure session handling
 201	- **Route Protection**: Protected routes for authenticated users
 202	- **Input Validation**: Server-side and client-side validation
 203	- **CSRF Protection**: Cross-site request forgery prevention
 204	
 205	## Payment Integration
 206	
 207	### **Supported Payment Methods**
 208	
 209	1. **Credit/Debit Cards**
 210	 - Real-time card number formatting
 211	 - Expiry date validation (MM/YY)
 212	 - CVV validation
 213	 - Cardholder name verification
 214	
 215	2. **UPI Payment**
 216	 - UPI ID validation
 217	 - Support for all major UPI apps
 218	 - Real-time payment processing
 219	
 220	3. **Digital Wallets**
 221	 - Paytm integration
 222	 - Amazon Pay support
 223	 - Mobikwik compatibility
 224	
 225	4. **Net Banking**
 226	 - Major Indian banks supported
 227	 - Secure bank redirections
 228	 - Real-time transaction status
 229	
 230	5. **Cash on Delivery**
 231	 - COD option with delivery notes
 232	 - Additional delivery time notifications
 233	
 234	### **Payment Security**
 235	- **Form Validation**: Real-time payment form validation
 236	- **Error Handling**: User-friendly error messages
 237	- **Order Tracking**: Complete order lifecycle management
 238	- **Receipt Generation**: Detailed order confirmations
 239	
 240	## Admin Dashboard Features
 241	
 242	### **Overview Dashboard**
 243	- **Real-time Metrics**: Live user counts, revenue, system health
 244	- **Trend Indicators**: Visual trend arrows with percentage changes
 245	- **Color-coded Cards**: Each metric category has unique styling
 246	- **Progress Visualizations**: Interactive progress bars
 247	
 248	### **Analytics Tab**
 249	- **Performance Metrics**: Conversion rates, customer satisfaction
 250	- **Real-time Activity**: Live system activity with pulse indicators
 251	- **Device Analytics**: Desktop/Mobile/Tablet usage breakdown
 252	- **Growth Tracking**: Monthly growth percentages
 253	
 254	### **User Management**
 255	- **Advanced Search**: Real-time user search functionality
 256	- **Smart Filters**: Filter by role and status
 257	- **Export Functionality**: Download user data
 258	- **User Actions**: Edit, activate/deactivate users
 259	
 260	### **System Monitoring**
 261	- **System Health**: CPU, Memory, Disk usage monitoring
 262	- **Security Dashboard**: Login attempts, API requests
 263	- **Live Counters**: Real-time system activity
 264	- **Configuration Access**: Quick system settings
 265	
 266	## Deployment
 267

 274	npm run preview

 287	- **Vercel**: Frontend deployment

 448	## Known Issues & Troubleshooting
 449	
 450	### **Common Issues**
 451	
 452	#### **Development Server Won't Start**
 453	```bash
 454	# Clear node_modules and reinstall
 455	rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json
 456	npm install
 457	npm run dev
 458	```
 459	
 460	#### **Port Already in Use**
 461	```bash
 462	# Kill process on port 5000
 463	npx kill-port 5000
 464	npm run dev
 465	```
 466	
 467	#### **TypeScript Errors**
 468	```bash
 469	# Run type checking
 470	npm run type-check
 471	# Fix any type errors before proceeding
 472	```
 473	
 474	#### **Build Failures**
 475	```bash
 476	# Clear build cache
 477	rm -rf dist
 478	npm run build
 479	```

 547	## API Documentation
 548	
 549	### **Authentication Endpoints**
 550	```typescript
 551	POST /api/auth/login // User login
 552	POST /api/auth/logout // User logout
 553	POST /api/auth/register // User registration
 554	GET /api/auth/profile // Get user profile
 555	```
 556	
 557	### **Product Endpoints**
 558	```typescript
 559	GET /api/products // Get all products
 560	GET /api/products/:id // Get product by ID
 561	POST /api/products // Create product (farmers)
 562	PUT /api/products/:id // Update product
 563	DELETE /api/products/:id // Delete product
 564	```
 565	
 566	### **Order Endpoints**
 567	```typescript
 568	POST /api/orders // Create new order
 569	GET /api/orders // Get user orders
 570	GET /api/orders/:id // Get order details
 571	PUT /api/orders/:id/status // Update order status
 572	```
 573	
 574	### **Admin Endpoints**
 575	```typescript
 576	GET /api/admin/stats // Dashboard statistics
 577	GET /api/admin/users // Get all users
 578	GET /api/admin/orders // Get all orders
 579	PUT /api/admin/users/:id // Update user status
 580	```

 1	import express, { type Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express";
 2	import { registerRoutes } from "./routes";
 3	import { setupVite, serveStatic, log } from "./vite";
 4	import dotenv from "dotenv";
 5	
 6	// Load environment variables from .env file
 7	dotenv.config();
 8	
 9	const app = express();
 10	app.use(express.json());
 11	app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: false }));
 12	
 13	app.use((req, res, next) => {
 14	 const start = Date.now();
 15	 const path = req.path;
 16	 let capturedJsonResponse: Record<string, any> | undefined = undefined;
 17	
 18	 const originalResJson = res.json;
 19	 res.json = function (bodyJson, ...args) {
 20	 capturedJsonResponse = bodyJson;
 21	 return originalResJson.apply(res, [bodyJson, ...args]);
 22	 };
 23	
 24	 res.on("finish", () => {
 25	 const duration = Date.now() - start;
 26	 if (path.startsWith("/api")) {
 27	 let logLine = `${req.method} ${path} ${res.statusCode} in ${duration}ms`;
 28	 if (capturedJsonResponse) {
 29	 logLine += ` :: ${JSON.stringify(capturedJsonResponse)}`;
 30	 }
 31	
 32	 if (logLine.length > 80) {
 33	 logLine = logLine.slice(0, 79) + "…";
 34	 }
 35	
 36	 log(logLine);
 37	 }
 38	 });
 39	
 40	 next();
 41	});
 42	
 43	(async () => {
 44	 const server = await registerRoutes(app);
 45	
 46	 app.use((err: any, _req: Request, res: Response, _next: NextFunction) => {
 47	 const status = err.status || err.statusCode || 500;
 48	 const message = err.message || "Internal Server Error";
 49	
 50	 res.status(status).json({ message });
 51	 throw err;
 52	 });
 53	
 54	 // importantly only setup vite in development and after
 55	 // setting up all the other routes so the catch-all route
 56	 // doesn't interfere with the other routes
 57	 if (app.get("env") === "development") {
 58	 await setupVite(app, server);
 59	 } else {
 60	 serveStatic(app);
 61	 }
 62	
 63	 // ALWAYS serve the app on port 5000
 64	 // this serves both the API and the client.
 65	 // It is the only port that is not firewalled.
 66	 const port = 5000;
 67	 server.listen({
 68	 port,
 69	 host: "127.0.0.1",
 70	 }, () => {
 71	 log(`serving on port ${port}`);
 72	 });
 73	})();
 74

 31	function Router() {

 10	Farm Connect is a modern, full-stack web application that bridges the gap between local farmers and consumers, promoting sustainable agriculture and fresh food access. Built with cutting-edge technologies and featuring a comprehensive admin dashboard, secure payment processing, and real-time features.

 21	
 22	// Generate JWT token
 23	export function generateToken(userId: number, username: string, role: string): string {
 24	 return jwt.sign(
 25	 { userId, username, role },
 26	 JWT_SECRET,
 27	 { expiresIn: JWT_EXPIRES_IN }
 28	 );
 29	}
 30	
 31	// Verify JWT token
 32	export function verifyToken(token: string): any {
 33	 try {
 34	 return jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET);
 35	 } catch (error) {
 36	 return null;
 37	 }
 38	}
 39	
 40	// Authentication middleware
 41	export function authenticate(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) {
 42	 // Get token from authorization header or cookie
 43	 const token = req.headers.authorization?.split(' ')[1] || req.cookies.token;
 44	 
 45	 if (!token) {
 46	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 47	 success: false, 
 48	 message: 'Authentication required. Please sign in.' 
 49	 });
 50	 }
 51	 
 52	 const decoded = verifyToken(token);
 53	 if (!decoded) {
 54	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 55	 success: false, 
 56	 message: 'Invalid or expired token. Please sign in again.' 
 57	 });
 58	 }
 59	 
 60	 // Add user data to request object
 61	 req.user = decoded;
 62	 next();
 63	}
 64	
 65	// Role-based authorization middleware
 66	export function authorize(roles: string[]) {
 67	 return (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
 68	 if (!req.user) {
 69	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 70	 success: false, 
 71	 message: 'Authentication required. Please sign in.' 
 72	 });
 73	 }
 74	 
 75	 if (!roles.includes(req.user.role)) {
 76	 return res.status(403).json({ 
 77	 success: false, 
 78	 message: 'Access denied. You do not have permission to access this resource.' 
 79	 });
 80	 }
 81	 
 82	 next();
 83	 };
 84	}

 140	// Handler for user login
 141	export async function loginHandler(req: Request, res: Response) {
 142	 try {
 143	 // Validate request body
 144	 const validatedData = loginUserSchema.parse(req.body);
 145	 
 146	 // Find user by username
 147	 const user = await storage.getUserByUsername(validatedData.username);
 148	 if (!user) {
 149	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 150	 success: false, 
 151	 message: 'Invalid username or password' 
 152	 });
 153	 }
 154	 
 155	 // Verify password
 156	 const isPasswordValid = await comparePassword(validatedData.password, user.password);
 157	 if (!isPasswordValid) {
 158	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 159	 success: false, 
 160	 message: 'Invalid username or password' 
 161	 });
 162	 }
 163	 
 164	 // Generate JWT token
 165	 const token = generateToken(user.id, user.username, user.role);
 166	 
 167	 // Set token in cookie (for web browsers)
 168	 res.cookie('token', token, {
 169	 httpOnly: true,
 170	 maxAge: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 24 hours
 171	 secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
 172	 sameSite: 'strict'
 173	 });
 174	 
 175	 // Return success response with token
 176	 res.status(200).json({
 177	 success: true,
 178	 message: 'Login successful',
 179	 token,
 180	 user: {
 181	 id: user.id,
 182	 username: user.username,
 183	 email: user.email,
 184	 firstName: user.firstName,
 185	 lastName: user.lastName,
 186	 role: user.role
 187	 }
 188	 });
 189	 } catch (error: any) {
 190	 console.error('Error logging in:', error);
 191	 res.status(400).json({ 
 192	 success: false, 
 193	 message: error.message || 'Failed to sign in' 
 194	 });
 195	 }
 196	}

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"3685b691-9823-471f-96d2-aa848613ad90","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":5,"content":"","tool_use":{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01QPoUwQYt5b1d8pxQU7NDmZ","tool_name":"str-replace-editor","input_json":"{"command": "str_replace", "path": "vercel.json", "instruction_reminder": "ALWAYS BREAK DOWN EDITS INTO SMALLER CHUNKS OF AT MOST 150 LINES EACH.", "old_str_1": "{\n \"version\": 2,\n \"builds\": [\n {\n \"src\": \"server/index.ts\",\n \"use\": \"@vercel/node\",\n \"config\": {\n \"includeFiles\": [\"server/**\", \"shared/**\", \"dist/**\"]\n }\n },\n {\n \"src\": \"package.json\",\n \"use\": \"@vercel/static-build\",\n \"config\": {\n \"distDir\": \"dist/public\"\n }\n }\n ],\n \"routes\": [\n {\n \"src\": \"/api/(.*)\",\n \"dest\": \"/server/index.ts\"\n },\n {\n \"src\": \"/(.*)\",\n \"dest\": \"/dist/public/$1\"\n }\n ],\n \"env\": {\n \"NODE_ENV\": \"production\"\n }\n}", "old_str_start_line_number_1": 1, "old_str_end_line_number_1": 33, "new_str_1": "{\n \"version\": 2,\n \"builds\": [\n {\n \"src\": \"server/index.ts\",\n \"use\": \"@vercel/node\",\n \"config\": {\n \"includeFiles\": [\"server/**\", \"shared/**\", \"dist/**\"]\n }\n },\n {\n \"src\": \"package.json\",\n \"use\": \"@vercel/static-build\",\n \"config\": {\n \"distDir\": \"dist/public\"\n }\n }\n ],\n \"routes\": [\n {\n \"src\": \"/api/(.*)\",\n \"dest\": \"/server/index.ts\"\n },\n {\n \"src\": \"/(.*\\\\.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|svg|ico|woff|woff2|ttf|eot))\",\n \"dest\": \"/dist/public/$1\"\n },\n {\n \"src\": \"/(.*)\",\n \"dest\": \"/dist/public/index.html\"\n }\n ],\n \"env\": {\n \"NODE_ENV\": \"production\"\n }\n}"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"Now I understand the issue! The problem is that this is a Single Page Application (SPA) using client-side routing with Wouter, but the Vercel configuration is not properly set up to handle client-side routes. When someone visits `/admin-login` directly, Vercel tries to find a static file at that path, but it doesn't exist because it's a client-side route.

Let me check the current Vercel configuration and then provide a solution:

## Problem Analysis

The issue is with the Vercel deployment configuration. Your app is a Single Page Application (SPA) that uses client-side routing with Wouter. The routes like `/admin-login` are handled by React Router on the client side, but when someone visits the URL directly (or refreshes the page), Vercel tries to serve a static file at that path, which doesn't exist.

## Solution

I need to update the `vercel.json` configuration to properly handle SPA routing by adding a fallback route that serves the `index.html` file for all non-API routes.

When someone visits `/admin-login` directly, Vercel tries to find a static file at that path, but it doesn't exist because it's a client-side route.

Let me check the current Vercel configuration and then provide a solution:

## Problem Analysis

The issue is with the Vercel deployment configuration. Your app is a Single Page Application (SPA) that uses client-side routing with Wouter. The routes like `/admin-login` are handled by React Router on the client side, but when someone visits the URL directly (or refreshes the page), Vercel tries to serve a static file at that path, which doesn't exist.

## Solution

I need to update the `vercel.json` configuration to properly handle SPA routing by adding a fallback route that serves the `index.html` file for all non-API routes.
","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"d8ac4daf-853b-4780-a54d-97601185e996","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"Perfect! I've updated the Vercel configuration to properly handle SPA routing. Here's what I changed:

## Changes Made

1. **Added static asset routing**: The new route `"/(.*\\.(js|css|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|svg|ico|woff|woff2|ttf|eot))"` ensures that static assets (JavaScript, CSS, images, fonts) are served directly from the `/dist/public/` directory.

2. **Added SPA fallback**: The catch-all route `"/(.*)"` now points to `/dist/public/index.html` instead of trying to serve files directly. This means that any route that doesn't match an API endpoint or static asset will serve the main `index.html` file, allowing React and Wouter to handle the client-side routing.

## How This Fixes the Issue

- When someone visits `/admin-login` directly, Vercel will now serve the `index.html` file
- React will load and Wouter will handle the routing to show the admin login page
- Static assets like CSS, JS, and images will still be served correctly
- API routes will continue to work as before

## Next Steps

You'll need to redeploy your application to Vercel for these changes to take effect. You can do this by:

1. Committing these changes to your repository
2. Pushing to the branch connected to your Vercel deployment
3. Or manually triggering a redeploy in the Vercel dashboard

After redeployment, the URL `https://farm-connect-aecvk58jc-vivek-bukkas-projects.vercel.app/admin-login` should work correctly and show your admin login page.

The admin login credentials are:
- Username: `admin`
- Password: `123456`

Would you like me to help you with anything else related to the deployment or the admin functionality?

 21	
 22	// Generate JWT token
 23	export function generateToken(userId: number, username: string, role: string): string {
 24	 return jwt.sign(
 25	 { userId, username, role },
 26	 JWT_SECRET,
 27	 { expiresIn: JWT_EXPIRES_IN }
 28	 );
 29	}
 30	
 31	// Verify JWT token
 32	export function verifyToken(token: string): any {
 33	 try {
 34	 return jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET);
 35	 } catch (error) {
 36	 return null;
 37	 }
 38	}
 39	
 40	// Authentication middleware
 41	export function authenticate(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) {
 42	 // Get token from authorization header or cookie
 43	 const token = req.headers.authorization?.split(' ')[1] || req.cookies.token;
 44	 
 45	 if (!token) {
 46	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 47	 success: false, 
 48	 message: 'Authentication required. Please sign in.' 
 49	 });
 50	 }
 51	 
 52	 const decoded = verifyToken(token);
 53	 if (!decoded) {
 54	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 55	 success: false, 
 56	 message: 'Invalid or expired token. Please sign in again.' 
 57	 });
 58	 }
 59	 
 60	 // Add user data to request object
 61	 req.user = decoded;
 62	 next();
 63	}
 64	
 65	// Role-based authorization middleware
 66	export function authorize(roles: string[]) {
 67	 return (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
 68	 if (!req.user) {
 69	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 70	 success: false, 
 71	 message: 'Authentication required. Please sign in.' 
 72	 });
 73	 }
 74	 
 75	 if (!roles.includes(req.user.role)) {
 76	 return res.status(403).json({ 
 77	 success: false, 
 78	 message: 'Access denied. You do not have permission to access this resource.' 
 79	 });
 80	 }
 81	 
 82	 next();
 83	 };
 84	}

 140	// Handler for user login
 141	export async function loginHandler(req: Request, res: Response) {
 142	 try {
 143	 // Validate request body
 144	 const validatedData = loginUserSchema.parse(req.body);
 145	 
 146	 // Find user by username
 147	 const user = await storage.getUserByUsername(validatedData.username);
 148	 if (!user) {
 149	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 150	 success: false, 
 151	 message: 'Invalid username or password' 
 152	 });
 153	 }
 154	 
 155	 // Verify password
 156	 const isPasswordValid = await comparePassword(validatedData.password, user.password);
 157	 if (!isPasswordValid) {
 158	 return res.status(401).json({ 
 159	 success: false, 
 160	 message: 'Invalid username or password' 
 161	 });
 162	 }
 163	 
 164	 // Generate JWT token
 165	 const token = generateToken(user.id, user.username, user.role);
 166	 
 167	 // Set token in cookie (for web browsers)
 168	 res.cookie('token', token, {
 169	 httpOnly: true,
 170	 maxAge: 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 24 hours
 171	 secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
 172	 sameSite: 'strict'
 173	 });
 174	 
 175	 // Return success response with token
 176	 res.status(200).json({
 177	 success: true,
 178	 message: 'Login successful',
 179	 token,
 180	 user: {
 181	 id: user.id,
 182	 username: user.username,
 183	 email: user.email,
 184	 firstName: user.firstName,
 185	 lastName: user.lastName,
 186	 role: user.role
 187	 }
 188	 });
 189	 } catch (error: any) {
 190	 console.error('Error logging in:', error);
 191	 res.status(400).json({ 
 192	 success: false, 
 193	 message: error.message || 'Failed to sign in' 
 194	 });
 195	 }
 196	}
